routes.rope_note: Short 5.5 rock scramble/climb on Red Mountain's ridge; single 60m rope sufficient for the low-5th-class sections, many parties simul-climb or scramble sections unroped.

sling_rack.notes: Rock reported loose in places; solid slabs on south side

routes.corrections: No route-specific gear beta found; rack inferred from 5.5 grade and terrain description.

routes.rope_note: Class 3-4 scramble, non-technical except a few hundred feet of 3rd/4th class near the true (South) summit; no rope used in standard dry-condition ascents.
routes.corrections: Grade/terrain-based inference; avalanche-exposed slopes noted for early-season snow.

routes.rope_note: Class 3 scramble; no rope required in dry conditions.
routes.corrections: No route-specific trip reports found beyond peak elevation (7,260ft); gear inferred purely from stated Class 3 grade.

routes.rope_note: Class 2 trail-based route via maintained Rock Mountain Trail #1587 (USFS) off US-2; no technical rock or rope gear needed.
routes.corrections: Confirmed via USFS trailhead page; maintained trail to near-summit scrambling only.

routes.rope_note: Grade II glacier route: roped glacier travel required crossing the Ruth Glacier (mandatory ice axe, strongly recommended rope especially after early July); parties commonly carry 30m ropes. Final approach to Icy Peak's summit is ~500ft of 2nd-3rd class, then a 100ft 4th-class summit gully typically climbed unroped/scrambled.

sling_rack.notes: Crevasse-rescue kit (prusik cords, pulley, carabiners) recommended, not a mechanical ascender

routes.corrections: Confirmed via multiple trip reports: glacier gear mandatory, crevasses open by late summer.

routes.rope_note: Class 2-3 scramble on the standard route; no rope typically needed away from any glaciated aspects (Fairchild Glacier sits on the NE slope, off standard line).
routes.corrections: Only general peak/geography info found; no dedicated route-level trip report distinguishing the standard scramble line from the glacier.

routes.rope_note: Class 2-3 scramble via broad gully to South Ridge; airy, exposed summit block with high rockfall danger and loose holds. No rope standard, though cautious parties may want a handline for the final exposed section.
routes.corrections: Confirmed via multiple route descriptions of the Emerald-Saska Col approach and South Ridge scramble.

routes.rope_note: Class 2-3 broad southwest-slope scramble from Leroy Creek Basin; boulder-hopping and loose granite but no roped climbing on the standard route.
routes.corrections: Confirmed via route descriptions; steep sections can be passed left or right without technical difficulty.

routes.rope_note: Short 5.6 trad route at Shark Rock (minor Gifford Pinchot crag); single 60m rope sufficient. Exact pitch count/length not published.

sling_rack.notes: Standard single-pitch trad rack for the grade; no route-specific beta found beyond MP grade listing

routes.corrections: Mountain Project confirms grade (5.6, trad) and area, but no published rack/pitch/length details.

routes.rope_note: 2 pitches, ~150ft total (5.10-, Grade II). Descent is two 75ft rappels from a fixed anchor at the top.

sling_rack.notes: Gear good once crack widens on P1; P2 gear sparse, best found in thin cracks/horizontal seams; two-bolt anchor atop P1

routes.corrections: Verified directly via Mountain Project route page (FA Sept 1990).

routes.rope_note: Mostly Class 3/4 rock and brush; one short pitch requires a rope belay going up, and the same section is rappelled off a tree anchor on descent. A 30m rope covers the single roped pitch.

sling_rack.slings: 1-2 long slings/webbing for the tree rap anchor
sling_rack.notes: Rarely visited, self-arrest same way up/down

routes.corrections: Confirmed via route description of the standard line from Weden Pass area.

routes.rope_note: Grade III, 5.10 multi-pitch alpine route on Silver Horn's south face (sub-summit of Silver Star). Single 60m rope appropriate for a route of this grade/length. Exact pitch count not published online.

sling_rack.cams: standard alpine rack to 3in, doubles in common mid sizes
sling_rack.notes: Clean white granite south face, comparable in character to the neighboring Chalice route on the same wall

routes.corrections: Primary trip report was blocked (403) from direct fetch; rack inferred by comparison to the well-documented neighboring Chalice route.

routes.rope_note: 8 pitches, 800ft (Grade III, 5.10-). Route follows flakes/corners/splitters to a mid-height ledge, then 3 more pitches to blocky summit terrain.

sling_rack.cams: standard alpine rack, doubles in common finger-to-hand sizes
sling_rack.notes: Standard alpine rack per Mountain Project; rarely climbed, no fixed gear noted

routes.corrections: Verified directly via Mountain Project route page.

routes.rope_note: 5.9+ ridge route on Silver Star Mountain (Okanogan); the closest well-documented comparable line (NE Ridge, Grade III 5.9) uses standard glacier-crossing gear plus a light rock rack for scrambling to the summit. A 50-60m single rope covers both glacier travel and short 5th-class ridge pitches.

sling_rack.notes: Approach/descent involves glacier or snowfield travel

routes.corrections: Primary source returned 403/certificate errors on fetch; inferred from the closely comparable, well-documented NE Ridge route character.

routes.rope_note: Class 4 with glacier crossing (Chickamin Glacier, approached via Ptarmigan Traverse). Rope up for glacier travel — crevassing and bergschrund can be serious.

sling_rack.pickets: snow pickets (bring extra) for Chickamin Glacier crossing
sling_rack.cams: minimal light rock rack for the summit scramble
sling_rack.notes: Gully described as one of the loosest/most unpleasant on the route

routes.corrections: Confirmed via multiple Ptarmigan Traverse trip reports.

routes.rope_note: Class 3-4 olivine scramble in the Twin Sisters Range; olivine rock is noted as unusually grippy, reducing rope necessity even on steeper sections.
routes.corrections: General Twin Sisters Range/Skookum Peak beta found but no route-specific trip report isolating the Skookum scramble line from roped climbs on adjacent Jaw's Tooth.

routes.rope_note: Class 2 scramble; described as non-technical, requiring more time/navigation than nearby peaks due to steep approach slopes, but no rope or technical gear needed.
routes.corrections: Confirmed non-technical standard approach from Stevens Pass PCT access.

routes.rope_note: 9 pitches, ~1100ft, Grade III 5.10 on Sloan Peak's Southwest Face (FA 9/11/2011). Single 60m rope standard for a route of this length/grade.

sling_rack.cams: medium alpine rack to 3+in, doubles recommended in common sizes
sling_rack.notes: Best climbed late season once large summit ledges shed snow

routes.corrections: FA trip report returned 403/blocked on fetch; grade/length/pitch-count confirmed via Mountain Project area listings, rack inferred by comparison to the neighboring Fire on the Mountain route.

routes.rope_note: 7 pitches of 5.10d rock (to a ledge intersecting the Corkscrew Route) plus ~500ft of 4th/low-5th scrambling to the true summit; ~1000ft technical + 500ft scrambling total. Generally good protection at cruxes but runout sections on P2 and P5.

sling_rack.notes: Lots of alpine draws recommended (doubles); a few extra mid-size pieces helpful on longer pitches

routes.corrections: Verified directly via Mountain Project route page and a corroborating trip report.

routes.rope_note: IV, 5.9+, 9 pitches, 1800ft. Lower half is easy low-angle slab (can be soloed/simul-climbed by confident parties), steepening higher up.

sling_rack.notes: 8-10 runners recommended, helmet mandatory; clean granite slabs with crack systems

routes.corrections: Verified via Mountain Project route page.
